_sources: country_population: UN World Population Prospects 2024, rounded (public). email_provider_share: approximate public figures (provider user counts / Statista email-client share), rounded DOWN-to-common (conservative, governor 3). name: coarse common/rare only — full name-frequency census tables are v1. All priors are deliberately conservative: when uncertain, p is rounded toward COMMON (fewer identifying bits) so the engine UNDER-claims resolution power, never over-claims.
_governors: 1) bits=-log2(p). 2) sum across DISTINCT axes only, one value per axis, correlated axes discounted/pick-one. 3) uncertain p → round toward common (fewer bits); unknown axis → 0 bits. 4) total capped < global_unique_bits; OSINT-only cap=cap_bits. 5) shadow only, descriptive.

name._note: coarse v0 — binary common/rare via collision_guard.COMMON_GIVEN_NAMES on the first token. p rounded toward common (conservative). full per-name census frequency is v1.

axis_correlations.note: ccTLD domain IS the country signal — see _CCTLD_TO_ISO gate

_axis_correlations_note: Generalized governor-2. For each declared {from,to} where BOTH axes are present, the dependent axis 'to' keeps bits*(1-lambda). lambda=1 => pick-one (to fully subsumed, bits=0). lambda=0 => independent (no-op). Single-level only: no transitive propagation (a->b and b->c does NOT cascade a->c). 'mode' optional: a named gate (e.g. ccTLD_match) that must pass for the discount to apply; absent mode => unconditional when both axes present.
_birth_year_note: S272 — coarse prior for a candidate_year extracted from a password (password_composition). default 0.013 ≈ 1/77 (uniform over ~77 active years, round-toward-common, governor 3). birth_year_prevalence can hold real per-year shares; empty = use default. A coarse year ~6.3 bits but flagged coarse so the UI never over-sells it.
